name: Neon Tetra
description: Brilliant iridescent blue and red. Peaceful schooling fish perfect for community tanks.

name: Cardinal Tetra
description: Deeper red than neons extending full body length. Stunning in blackwater setups.

name: Rummy Nose Tetra
description: Bright red nose with striped tail. Tight schooling behavior is mesmerizing.

name: Ember Tetra
description: Tiny glowing orange jewels. Perfect for nano planted tanks.

name: Black Neon Tetra
description: Elegant black and iridescent green stripe. Hardy and underrated.

name: Congo Tetra
description: Shimmering rainbow iridescence with flowing fins. A showstopper in groups.

name: Serpae Tetra
description: Deep red body with black diamond marking. Can be nippy — keep in larger groups.

name: Glowlight Tetra
description: Translucent body with glowing orange-red stripe. Peaceful and elegant.

name: Green Neon Tetra
description: Smaller cousin of the neon with more green-blue iridescence. Loves soft water.

name: Diamond Tetra
description: Sparkling scales that flash like diamonds under aquarium lights.

name: Lemon Tetra
description: Translucent yellow body with bright eye. Very peaceful community fish.

name: Bloodfin Tetra
description: Silver body with vivid red fins. Extremely hardy and long-lived.

name: Buenos Aires Tetra
description: Larger tetra with red fins. Very hardy but may eat live plants.

name: Emperor Tetra
description: Dark body with bright blue eyes and sweeping tail fins. Regal appearance.

name: Silvertip Tetra
description: Active schooler with silver-tipped fins. Great mid-water swimmer.

name: Cherry Barb
description: Vibrant red coloration. Hardy, peaceful, and great for planted tanks.

name: Tiger Barb
description: Bold orange and black stripes. Semi-aggressive — keep in groups of 6+ to reduce fin nipping.

name: Odessa Barb
description: Males display an intense crimson red stripe. Underappreciated gem.

name: Gold Barb
description: Bright golden-yellow body. Very peaceful and hardy for cooler tanks.

name: Rosy Barb
description: Rosy pink males with green sheen. Tolerates cooler temperatures.

name: Checker Barb
description: Checkerboard scale pattern. Peaceful alternative to tiger barbs.

name: Denison Barb
description: Torpedo-shaped with red and black racing stripes. Needs swimming space.

name: Five-Banded Barb
description: Five distinct vertical bands. Peaceful and attractive schooler.

name: Harlequin Rasbora
description: Copper and black wedge pattern. Peaceful and looks stunning in groups.

name: Chili Rasbora
description: Tiny ruby-red fish perfect for nano tanks. Thrives in soft acidic water.

name: Galaxy Rasbora (Celestial Pearl Danio)
description: Deep blue body covered in pearl-like spots with red fins. Nano tank superstar.

name: Espei Rasbora (Lambchop)
description: Copper body with lambchop-shaped marking. Peaceful and attractive.

name: Scissortail Rasbora
description: Distinctive fork-tail with black and white markings. Active swimmer.

name: Exclamation Point Rasbora
description: Tiny with exclamation mark body pattern. Great for nano setups.

name: Phoenix Rasbora
description: Fiery orange coloration on a tiny body. Soft water preferred.

name: Zebra Danio
description: Classic horizontal stripes. Incredibly hardy and active. Great beginner fish.

name: Pearl Danio
description: Pearlescent sheen with orange accents. Hardy and peaceful.

name: Celestial Pearl Danio
description: Galaxy-patterned micro fish. Stunning in planted nano tanks.

name: Giant Danio
description: Large active danio with blue and gold stripes. Needs lots of swimming room.

name: White Cloud Mountain Minnow
description: Colorful coldwater fish. Red fins and iridescent body. No heater needed.

name: Guppy
description: Endlessly colorful males with flowing tails. Breed prolifically.

name: Endler's Livebearer
description: Tiny vibrant males with wild neon patterns. Prolific breeders.

name: Platy
description: Chunky colorful livebearers in every color imaginable. Extremely easy to keep.

name: Swordtail
description: Males sport an iconic sword-shaped tail extension. Active and colorful.

name: Molly
description: Available in black, white, dalmatian, and more. Appreciates some salt.

name: Least Killifish
description: One of the smallest livebearers. Great for nano tanks.

name: German Blue Ram
description: Electric blue and gold. A dwarf cichlid with incredible personality.

name: Bolivian Ram
description: Hardier cousin of the German Blue Ram. Great personality in a peaceful package.

name: Apistogramma Cacatuoides
description: Cockatoo dwarf cichlid with flamboyant dorsal fin rays. Full of attitude.

name: Apistogramma Borellii
description: Powder blue males with yellow accents. One of the easier apistos.

name: Kribensis
description: Purple belly during breeding. One of the easiest cichlids to keep.

name: Keyhole Cichlid
description: Peaceful cichlid with keyhole-shaped marking. Shy but charming.

name: Yellow Lab (Labidochromis caeruleus)
description: Bright electric yellow. One of the least aggressive mbuna.

name: Red Zebra
description: Vivid orange-red mbuna. Active and assertive personality.

name: Demasoni
description: Deep blue with black barring. Must be overstocked to spread aggression.

name: Peacock Cichlid (Aulonocara)
description: Brilliant metallic blues, reds, and yellows. More peaceful than mbuna.

name: Frontosa
description: Majestic Tanganyikan giant with a prominent forehead hump. Slow and regal.

name: Shell Dweller (Neolamprologus multifasciatus)
description: Tiny cichlid that lives in empty snail shells. Fascinating social behavior.

name: Tropheus Duboisi
description: Juveniles have white spots that transform into a banded adult pattern.

name: Oscar
description: Dog-like personality. Recognizes its owner. Grows large and messy but lovable.

name: Jack Dempsey
description: Iridescent blue spangles on a dark body. Named for the famous boxer.

name: Electric Blue Acara
description: Stunning metallic blue. One of the most peaceful medium-sized cichlids.

name: Firemouth Cichlid
description: Flares its bright red throat when displaying. More bark than bite.

name: Green Terror
description: Metallic green and blue with orange-edged fins. Can be aggressive.

name: Severum
description: Disc-shaped with beautiful banding. Calmer alternative to more aggressive cichlids.

name: Dwarf Gourami
description: Brilliant blues and reds. A labyrinth fish that breathes air from the surface.

name: Pearl Gourami
description: Lace-like pearl pattern across the body. One of the most beautiful gouramis.

name: Honey Gourami
description: Warm honey-gold coloration. Smaller and more peaceful than dwarf gouramis.

name: Sparkling Gourami
description: Tiny gourami that makes audible croaking sounds. Sparkling blue eyes.

name: Three Spot Gourami
description: Classic gourami in blue, gold, and opaline varieties. Hardy and adaptable.

name: Chocolate Gourami
description: Rich chocolate brown with gold stripes. Requires very soft acidic water.

name: Giant Gourami
description: Grows up to 2 feet. Recognizes owners and has dog-like personality.

name: Betta Splendens
description: Stunning flowing fins. Keep males solo — territorial but full of personality.

name: Betta Imbellis
description: Peaceful wild betta. Can sometimes be kept in pairs. Subtle iridescent beauty.

name: Betta Albimarginata
description: Mouthbrooding betta with red-edged fins. Can be kept in groups.

name: Corydoras Catfish
description: Adorable bottom-dwellers that keep your substrate clean. Very social.

name: Pygmy Corydoras
description: Tiny cories that swim in midwater. Adorable in large groups.

name: Panda Corydoras
description: Black and white panda markings. Prefers slightly cooler temperatures.

name: Sterbai Corydoras
description: White spots on dark body with orange pectoral fins. Heat-tolerant cory.

name: Bristlenose Pleco
description: Excellent algae eater. Stays small unlike common plecos.

name: Clown Pleco
description: Small pleco with bold striping pattern. Eats driftwood.

name: Otocinclus
description: Tiny algae eating machines. Sensitive at first but excellent once established.

name: Whiptail Catfish
description: Bizarre elongated body and tail. A fascinating conversation starter.

name: Glass Catfish
description: Completely transparent body showing skeleton and organs. Unique.

name: Upside-Down Catfish
description: Swims belly-up. One of the most amusing fish to watch.

name: Pictus Catfish
description: Sleek silver body with black spots and long whiskers. Active at night.

name: Kuhli Loach
description: Snake-like striped loach that hides during the day. Adorable noodle fish.

name: Clown Loach
description: Bold orange and black bands. Playful and social but grows very large.

name: Hillstream Loach
description: Flattened body clings to rocks in fast flow. Looks like a tiny stingray.

name: Yoyo Loach
description: Y-O-Y-O pattern on body. Active, social, and great snail control.

name: Dojo/Weather Loach
description: Becomes active before storms. Friendly eel-like fish that tolerates cold.

name: Dwarf Chain Loach
description: Beautiful chain-link pattern. Active during the day unlike most loaches.

name: Zebra Loach
description: Tight zebra stripes. Peaceful and stays a manageable size.

name: Angelfish
description: Majestic and graceful. A stunning centerpiece fish for medium to large tanks.

name: Discus
description: The king of freshwater aquariums. Requires pristine water but rewards with breathtaking beauty.

name: Altum Angelfish
description: Taller and more wild-looking than common angels. Requires soft acidic water.

name: Clown Killifish
description: Tiny surface dweller with rocket-shaped tail. Brilliant blue and red.

name: Norman's Lampeye
description: Glowing blue eye tops visible even in dim light. Delicate but beautiful.

name: Golden Wonder Killifish
description: Metallic gold surface predator. Will eat very small fish but otherwise peaceful.

name: Gardneri Killifish
description: Stunning blue and red patterns. One of the most colorful freshwater fish.

name: Boesemani Rainbow
description: Half blue, half orange. Males color up spectacularly with age.

name: Neon Dwarf Rainbow
description: Compact rainbow with neon blue shimmer. Perfect for smaller tanks.

name: Turquoise Rainbow
description: Deep turquoise body that intensifies with maturity. Needs a group.

name: Threadfin Rainbow
description: Males have incredible elongated fin filaments. Delicate and stunning.

name: Celebes Rainbow
description: Translucent body with neon blue line and sail-like fins. Unique beauty.

name: Red Irian Rainbow
description: Deep red coloration in mature males. Active and social.

name: Paradise Fish
description: Stunning red and blue stripes. Hardy enough for unheated tanks. Can be feisty.

name: Wrestling Halfbeak
description: Needle-nosed surface dweller. Males 'wrestle' with locked jaws.

name: Scarlet Badis
description: One of the smallest known fish. Males are scarlet red with blue iridescence.

name: Pea Puffer
description: World's smallest pufferfish. Tiny but packed with personality and intelligence.

name: Figure Eight Puffer
description: Brackish puffer with figure-8 marking on back. Curious and interactive.

name: Rope Fish
description: Snake-like oddball with tiny pectoral fins. Peaceful nocturnal predator.

name: Elephant Nose Fish
description: Uses electrical pulses to navigate. Trunk-like mouth probe is fascinating.

name: African Butterfly Fish
description: Surface dweller with wing-like pectoral fins. Ancient-looking predator.

name: Siamese Algae Eater
description: True algae eater that consumes black beard algae. Active swimmer.

name: Chinese Algae Eater
description: Good algae eater when young. Can become territorial with age.

name: Flying Fox
description: Often confused with SAE. Eats some algae and is generally peaceful.

name: Archer Fish
description: Shoots water jets to knock insects off plants above water. Brackish preferred.

name: Bichir (Senegal)
description: Prehistoric dinosaur fish with armored scales and lung-like swim bladder.

name: Red Tail Shark
description: Jet black body with vibrant red tail. Territorial bottom dweller — keep one only.

name: Rainbow Shark
description: Dark body with red-orange fins. Less aggressive than red tail shark but still territorial.

name: Hatchetfish
description: Deep-bodied surface fish shaped like a hatchet. Can actually fly short distances.

name: Pencilfish
description: Slender hovering fish that rests at an angle. Peaceful and subtle beauty.

name: Ricefish (Medaka)
description: Hardy little fish popular in Asia for centuries. Many color varieties. Cool water ok.

name: Vampire Pleco (L029)
description: Striking black and white banding. Needs driftwood and some current.

name: Ocellaris Clownfish
description: The iconic 'Nemo'. Hardy, colorful, and bonds with anemones.

name: Percula Clownfish
description: True percula with thicker black outlines. Slightly more demanding than ocellaris.

name: Maroon Clownfish
description: Largest and most aggressive clownfish. Deep maroon with gold or white stripes.

name: Clarkii Clownfish
description: Hardy and adaptable. Accepts many different anemone species as hosts.

name: Royal Gramma
description: Striking purple and yellow bicolor. A peaceful reef-safe cave dweller.

name: Yellow Tang
description: Electric yellow coloration. Active swimmer needing room to roam.

name: Blue Hippo Tang
description: The real 'Dory'. Vibrant blue with black palette marking. Ich-prone.

name: Kole Tang
description: Brown body with fine horizontal striping. Excellent algae grazer for reefs.

name: Tomini Tang
description: Smaller tang suitable for medium tanks. Great algae control.

name: Firefish Goby
description: Elegant white body with fiery red tail. Peaceful and hardy. Needs a lid.

name: Purple Firefish
description: Purple-magenta variant of the firefish. Stunning but shy.

name: Watchman Goby
description: Partners with pistol shrimp in a symbiotic relationship. Fascinating behavior.

name: Clown Goby
description: Tiny goby that perches on coral branches. Comes in yellow, green, and citron.

name: Mandarin Dragonet
description: Perhaps the most beautiful marine fish. Psychedelic patterns. Needs copepod population.

name: Six Line Wrasse
description: Six horizontal purple and orange lines. Active pest controller.

name: Fairy Wrasse (Carpenter's)
description: Spectacular multicolored display during flashing. Peaceful reef fish.

name: Melanurus Wrasse
description: Colorful pest eater. Consumes flatworms, bristleworms, and pyramidellid snails.

name: Yellow Coris Wrasse
description: Bright yellow with a fun swimming style. Buries in sand at night.

name: Flame Angelfish
description: Fiery red-orange with black bars. May nip at corals — reef safe with caution.

name: Coral Beauty Angelfish
description: Deep purple and orange. One of the most popular dwarf angels.

name: Lemonpeel Angelfish
description: Bright lemon yellow with blue eye ring. May nip corals.

name: Bicolor Blenny
description: Half purple, half orange. Perches on rocks with comical expressions.

name: Midas Blenny
description: Golden yellow eel-shaped blenny. Hovers above rockwork with personality.

name: Tailspot Blenny
description: Tiny algae-eating blenny with a distinctive tail spot. Perfect for nano reefs.

name: Bangaii Cardinalfish
description: Striking black and silver with elongated fins. Mouthbrooding paternal care.

name: Pajama Cardinalfish
description: Polka dots, stripes, and big eyes. Looks like it's wearing pajamas.

name: Chromis (Blue-Green)
description: Peaceful schooling damsel. Iridescent blue-green shimmer in groups.

name: Yellowtail Damsel
description: Electric blue body with yellow tail. Hardy but can become territorial.

name: Flame Hawkfish
description: Brilliant red with black markings around eyes. Perches on corals and watches.

name: Longnose Hawkfish
description: Checkered red and white with elongated snout. Charismatic percher.

name: Foxface Rabbitfish
description: Yellow body with black and white fox-like face. Venomous spines. Great algae eater.

name: Copperband Butterflyfish
description: Elegant vertical copper bands with long snout. Eats aiptasia anemones.

name: Dottyback (Orchid)
description: Vivid purple. Captive bred varieties are much less aggressive than wild.

name: Neon Goby
description: Tiny neon blue-striped goby. Cleans parasites from larger fish.

name: Engineer Goby
description: Black and white striped burrower. Creates elaborate tunnel systems.

name: Lawnmower Blenny
description: Constantly grazes algae off rocks. Comical face and entertaining behavior.

name: Amano Shrimp
description: Champion algae eaters. Transparent with distinctive dots along the body.

name: Cherry Shrimp
description: Brilliant red neocaridina. Breed readily and create living jewels in your tank.

name: Blue Dream Shrimp
description: Deep blue neocaridina variety. Just as hardy as cherry shrimp.

name: Orange Sakura Shrimp
description: Vivid orange neocaridina. Great contrast against green plants.

name: Yellow Shrimp
description: Bright yellow neocaridina. Cheerful and easy to breed.

name: Crystal Red Shrimp
description: Red and white banded caridina. Requires very specific soft acidic water.

name: Crystal Black Shrimp
description: Black and white version of crystal red. Same demanding water requirements.

name: Taiwan Bee Shrimp
description: Premium shrimp varieties including King Kong, Panda, and Red Wine.

name: Bamboo Shrimp
description: Filter feeding shrimp with fan-like hands. Sits in current catching food particles.

name: Vampire Shrimp
description: Large blue-gray filter feeder. Impressive armored appearance.

name: Ghost Shrimp
description: Nearly transparent and very affordable. Great cleanup crew on a budget.

name: Whisker Shrimp
description: Larger than ghost shrimp with prominent whiskers. Can be slightly aggressive.

name: Nerite Snail
description: Tireless algae eaters with beautiful shell patterns. Won't breed in freshwater.

name: Mystery Snail
description: Colorful and entertaining to watch. Available in gold, blue, ivory, and more.

name: Rabbit Snail
description: Elongated snail from Sulawesi with a rabbit-like face. Slow and peaceful.

name: Assassin Snail
description: Hunts and eats pest snails. A biological control for snail infestations.

name: Japanese Trapdoor Snail
description: Hardy livebearing snail. Good algae eater that won't overpopulate.

name: Ramshorn Snail
description: Flat spiral shell in pink, blue, or red. Prolific breeders and good cleaners.

name: Malaysian Trumpet Snail
description: Burrows in substrate keeping it aerated. Mostly nocturnal. Breeds readily.

name: Mexican Dwarf Crayfish (CPO)
description: Tiny bright orange crayfish. Generally safe with most fish unlike larger crayfish.

name: Blue Crayfish (Procambarus alleni)
description: Electric blue freshwater lobster. Will eat plants and slow fish.

name: Thai Micro Crab
description: Tiny fully aquatic crabs smaller than a dime. Delicate filter feeders.

name: Freshwater Clam
description: Filter feeds water keeping it crystal clear. Needs established tank with food particles.

name: Cleaner Shrimp (Skunk)
description: Sets up cleaning stations for fish. Charismatic and useful reef inhabitant.

name: Fire Shrimp
description: Blood red body with white spots and antennae. Stunning and functional.

name: Peppermint Shrimp
description: Red and white striped. Eats pest aiptasia anemones.

name: Coral Banded Shrimp
description: Red and white banded with long white antennae. Can catch small fish.

name: Sexy Shrimp
description: Tiny shrimp that sways its tail seductively. Lives on anemones.

name: Harlequin Shrimp
description: Spectacular purple and white. Feeds exclusively on starfish.

name: Pistol Shrimp (Randall's)
description: Partners with watchman gobies. Creates burrows and produces snapping sounds.

name: Emerald Crab
description: Brilliant green. Consumes bubble algae that other creatures won't touch.

name: Blue Leg Hermit Crab
description: Tiny algae-eating hermit. Essential cleanup crew member for reef tanks.

name: Scarlet Reef Hermit
description: Bright red legs. Peaceful herbivore that won't bother tankmates.

name: Halloween Hermit Crab
description: Orange and black legs like Halloween colors. Larger but generally peaceful.

name: Turbo Snail
description: Powerhouse algae eaters. Can knock over loose corals with their bulk.

name: Astrea Snail
description: Efficient algae grazers. Can't right themselves if flipped — check on them.

name: Nassarius Snail
description: Sand-sifting scavenger that burrows and pops up when food is detected.

name: Cerith Snail
description: Elongated spiral shell. Excellent detritus and algae eater. Works at night.

name: Trochus Snail
description: Can right itself unlike astrea. Hardy and effective algae grazer.

name: Fighting Conch
description: Sand-sifting conch with beautiful shell. Keeps sandbed clean.

name: Tuxedo Urchin
description: Red and black banded urchin. Decorates itself with shells and debris. Great algae eater.

name: Pencil Urchin
description: Thick blunt spines. Hardy urchin but may munch on some corals.

name: Sand Sifting Starfish
description: Processes sand for food. Can deplete sandbed fauna in small tanks.

name: Serpent Starfish
description: Long flexible arms that reach from crevices to grab food. Excellent scavenger.

name: Porcelain Crab
description: Flat filter-feeding crab that lives in anemones alongside clownfish.

name: Arrow Crab
description: Long spindly legs and pointed nose. Eats bristleworms but may grab small fish.

name: Feather Duster Worm
description: Beautiful fan-shaped crown extends from a tube to filter feed. Retracts when startled.

name: Maxima Clam
description: Brilliant electric blue and green mantles. Requires intense lighting.

name: Derasa Clam
description: Largest and hardiest of the giant clams for aquariums. Less light demanding.

name: Zoanthids
description: Kaleidoscopic colors. The gateway coral — hardy and comes in infinite morphs.

name: Mushroom Coral (Discosoma)
description: Soft flowing discs in vivid colors. Nearly indestructible for a coral.

name: Rhodactis Mushroom
description: Hairy/bumpy mushroom variety. Can grow very large. Many color morphs.

name: Ricordea Florida
description: Bubble-like tentacles in orange, green, blue. Premium collector coral.

name: Ricordea Yuma
description: More demanding than Florida ricordea. Extraordinary color combinations.

name: Green Star Polyps (GSP)
description: Bright green flowing polyps on purple mat. Grows fast — can be invasive.

name: Xenia (Pulsing)
description: Hypnotic pulsing motion. Grows rapidly and pulses to exchange gases.

name: Kenya Tree Coral
description: Tree-shaped soft coral that drops branches to propagate. Nearly unkillable.

name: Toadstool Leather Coral
description: Mushroom-shaped with flowing polyps. Periodically sheds a waxy coating.

name: Devil's Hand Leather
description: Finger-like lobes with tiny polyps. Very hardy and grows large.

name: Finger Leather Coral
description: Branching finger-like projections. Hardy and adds great texture.

name: Cabbage Leather Coral
description: Ruffled edges like a head of cabbage. Dramatic size and presence.

name: Clove Polyps
description: Delicate eight-tentacled polyps in brown, white, or green. Spreads moderately.

name: Anthelia
description: Waving hand-like polyps. Some varieties pulse like xenia. Easy grower.

name: Blue Clove Polyps
description: Metallic blue polyps that spread across rockwork. Stunning under blue LEDs.

name: Hammer Coral (Euphyllia)
description: Flowing tentacles with hammer-shaped tips. Mesmerizing movement under flow.

name: Torch Coral (Euphyllia)
description: Long flowing tentacles. Clownfish sometimes host in these as anemone substitutes.

name: Frogspawn Coral (Euphyllia)
description: Bubble-tipped tentacles resembling frog eggs. Beautiful in large colonies.

name: Duncan Coral
description: Round polyps on branching stalks. Fast growing and very forgiving LPS.

name: Candy Cane Coral
description: Trumpet-shaped polyps in pastel colors. Great beginner LPS coral.

name: Acan Lord (Micromussa)
description: Fleshy circular polyps in rainbow colors. Highly collectible morphs.

name: Brain Coral (Favia/Favites)
description: Boulder-shaped with maze-like pattern. Extends sweeper tentacles at night.

name: Blastomussa
description: Fleshy polyps in reds and greens. Low light tolerant and very easy LPS.

name: Goniopora (Flowerpot)
description: Beautiful daisy-like polyps. Historically difficult but captive varieties improving.

name: Bubble Coral
description: Inflates grape-like bubbles during the day. Deflates at night to feed.

name: Plate Coral (Fungia)
description: Free-living coral that sits on sand. Can actually move slowly across substrate.

name: Elegance Coral
description: Magnificent flowing tentacles with colored tips. Demanding but breathtaking.

name: Lobo Brain Coral
description: Smooth fleshy brain coral. Hardy and comes in many color combinations.

name: Chalice Coral
description: Encrusting plates with incredible eye patterns. Highly sought-after morphs.

name: War Coral (Favites)
description: Named for aggressive sweeper tentacles at night. Beautiful honeycomb pattern.

name: Scoly (Scolymia)
description: Single large polyp in stunning rainbow colors. Premium collector coral.

name: Wellsophyllia
description: Folded brain-like appearance in greens and reds. Dramatic under blue lights.

name: Acropora
description: The pinnacle of reef keeping. Stunning branching structures demanding pristine water.

name: Montipora (Plating)
description: Plating growth form in vivid colors. Easier SPS — great stepping stone to acropora.

name: Montipora (Digitata)
description: Finger-like branching montipora. Fast growing and colorful.

name: Birdsnest Coral (Seriatopora)
description: Fine delicate branches in pink, green, or blue. One of the easiest SPS.

name: Pocillopora
description: Cauliflower-like growth. Very fast growing SPS. Good beginner SPS.

name: Stylophora
description: Smooth branching coral in bright pinks and greens. Moderately easy SPS.

name: Pavona
description: Lettuce-like encrusting coral. Grows under a wide range of conditions.

name: Oregon Tort Acropora
description: Famous vivid blue acropora. One of the most sought-after SPS morphs.

name: Walt Disney Acropora
description: Multicolored showpiece with blue base, purple branches, and green polyps.

name: Bubble Tip Anemone
description: Most popular host anemone for clownfish. Comes in green, rose, and rainbow.

name: Rock Flower Anemone
description: Colorful Caribbean anemone. Stays put and doesn't wander like other nems.

name: Magnificent Anemone
description: Massive and stunning but demanding. The preferred host for ocellaris clownfish.

name: Long Tentacle Anemone
description: Graceful flowing tentacles rooted in sand. Can wander and sting corals.

name: African Dwarf Frog
description: Fully aquatic and full of charm. Loves to float at the surface with arms spread.

name: Axolotl
description: The smiling salamander. Needs cold water and gentle flow. Utterly unique.

name: Fire Belly Newt
description: Vivid orange belly warns predators. Semi-aquatic — needs land area access.

name: Paddle Tail Newt
description: Fully aquatic newt with a paddle-shaped tail for swimming. Nocturnal.

name: Eastern Newt
description: Olive green aquatic adult stage. Goes through a bright orange eft land phase.

name: African Clawed Frog
description: Larger cousin of dwarf frogs. Fully aquatic but will eat anything that fits in its mouth.

name: Spanish Ribbed Newt
description: Can push its ribs through its skin as a defense. Hardy and long-lived.

name: Alpine Newt
description: Stunning blue and orange breeding colors. Needs cool temperatures.

name: Surinam Toad
description: Bizarre flat toad. Babies develop in pockets on mother's back. Fully aquatic.

name: Japanese Fire Belly Newt
description: Bright red belly with dark top. Hardy and long-lived — can exceed 25 years.

name: Mexican Axolotl (Wild Type)
description: Dark wild-type coloration. Critically endangered in the wild.

name: Axolotl (Leucistic)
description: White/pink body with dark eyes. The most popular axolotl morph.

name: Axolotl (GFP/Green Fluorescent)
description: Glows green under blue/UV light. A unique and eye-catching morph.
